entry.title: Unlock Better Hiring with Qualifications: Start Smart, Scale Faster with SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
Why Qualifications Matter

Qualifications bring structure and consistency to hiring by replacing free-text job requirements with clearly defined skills. When used consistently, they improve candidate matching, enable better system recommendations, and create a reusable framework that scales with your organization. Without this structure, job postings vary widely and AI features lack the context needed to be effective.

The Hidden Cost of Skipping Day 1 Setup

Features like Recommend Qualifications depend entirely on an existing qualification library. Without it, the system has no reference point, and recommendations cannot work. Creating this library manually can feel time-consuming, which is why many teams delay it—and often never return to it.

The Solution: Generate Qualifications from Existing Data

Instead of starting from scratch, you can use Generate Qualifications to build your library instantly using AI. The system analyzes your existing job postings and templates, extracts commonly used skills, and creates a recommended list of qualifications. In one step, you move from having no structure to a usable, standardized framework.

What You Need to Do (Day One Setup)

  1. Upload your job postings or templates
  2. Contact your Configuration Manager to run Generate Qualifications
  3. Review and select the relevant qualifications

This creates a ready-to-use qualification library that supports both job postings and recommendations.

Activating Recommend Qualifications

Once your library is in place, enable the Recommend Qualifications feature. During job posting creation, the system suggests relevant qualifications based on your job description—using your own structured data.

Key insight: The quality of recommendations depends on the quality of your library.

How This Improves Hiring

With qualifications in place, recruiters can create consistent job postings, rely on better recommendations, and improve candidate matching. This leads to faster, more accurate hiring decisions without adding complexity.

Final Thoughts

Qualifications are a foundational step in skill-based hiring. By setting them up early using Generate Qualifications, you can quickly unlock the value of AI features and create a more consistent, scalable hiring process.

Start with the data you already have, build your qualification library, and let the system do the rest.

You can also have a look at the following topic in the What’s New Viewer for SAP Fieldglass for more details: 

AI Assisted Qualification Generation for Qualification Library

entry.title: CX Onboarding - New Sales Cloud and Service Cloud V2 Playlist on AI
entry.content:
New Sales Cloud and Service Cloud V2 Playlist on AI: How AI Is Transforming Customer Experience

The business world is changing fast, and customer expectations are higher than ever. Today, customers expect seamless, personalized experiences at every touchpoint—and artificial intelligence is no longer a nice-to-have, but a fundamental capability to deliver them.

AI is transforming how organizations operate by enabling them to better understand customer behavior, anticipate needs, and respond with precision. What was once reactive and manual is becoming proactive and intelligent, allowing businesses to move faster, make better decisions, and create more meaningful connections with their customers.

At SAP, this transformation goes beyond isolated AI use cases. It is part of a broader vision: the Autonomous Enterprise. In this model, end-to-end business processes are powered by AI and organized across core functions such as Finance, Supply Chain, Procurement, Human Capital Management, and Customer Experience. AI agents operate across these domains, executing complex workflows autonomously while maintaining enterprise governance and control.

Within this vision, Customer Experience plays a central role. Autonomous CX brings together CRM and customer experience processes into a unified system, where AI acts on a complete, real-time view of your business. This enables organizations to deliver consistent, intelligent, and highly personalized experiences at scale.

This transformation becomes tangible in solutions like SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2, where AI is embedded directly into everyday workflows.

In customer service, AI helps teams move from information overload to instant expertise. Capabilities such as case management assistance streamline case preparation, processing, and resolution while learning from every interaction—turning expert knowledge into reusable insights for the entire team.

In sales, AI enables a shift from data overload to guided selling. Intelligent assistants help prioritize opportunities, identify risks early, and automate time-consuming tasks like quote generation, allowing sales teams to focus on what matters most: closing deals and building relationships.

Together, these capabilities represent a fundamental shift in how Customer Experience is delivered in both efficiency and better outcomes for customers.

As AI adoption continues to accelerate, many organizations are looking for practical ways to get started and realize value quickly—especially during onboarding and implementation phases.

To support our customers on this journey, the CX Onboarding team has created a 📺comprehensive video playlist for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2. This playlist is designed to introduce the key AI capabilities available within the solutions and help you accelerate adoption, maximize ROI, and build a strong foundation for success.

👇Read below to learn more.

Introduction to SAP Business AI

Learn why AI is more important than ever and about SAP’s integrated approach to Customer Experience and intelligent applications. It shows how SAP connects CX with backend systems through a unified data layer, enabling real-time insights and seamless collaboration across business functions.

Joule, SAP’s Business Copilot

Get introduced to Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, and how it enables natural, conversational interaction with business systems. Joule helps users access information quickly, automate tasks, and navigate to the right applications, all while integrating with tools like Microsoft 365.

AI Agents for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2

This video explains what AI agents are and how they operate autonomously to perform tasks with minimal human intervention. It showcases use cases such as automatic case classification and a Q&A agent that improves response accuracy and consistency.

Generative AI Features for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2

This session focuses on generative AI and how it creates new content based on learned patterns. It demonstrates features such as Lead Booster, which enhances customer understanding, and the Email Draft Recommender, which helps streamline communication.

Machine Learning Features for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2

Explore how machine learning helps systems learn from data and improve over time. Become familiar with its capabilities such as Lead Intelligence, which predicts conversion likelihood, and Case Topic Analyzer, which organizes case data into meaningful insights.

🔍Explore our playlist to learn how to make the most out of AI with Sales Cloud and Service Cloud V2.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Thank you for reading this blog. We hope you found the content insightful. Please give us a like or leave a comment; we’d love to hear from you!

You can request a one‑on‑one call with one of our onboarding advisors using this form. 

💻Want to get in touch with us? Send an email to SAP_CX_Onboarding@sap.com.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

👉Key Resources

  • Onboarding Resource Center: SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
  • AI guide for Sales Cloud and Service Cloud V2
  • Joule Agents and Joule Assistants for customer experience
  • SAP Business AI in the Discovery Center

entry.title: Building a Native MCP Server in SAP ABAP: Enabling AI Agents to Talk to Your SAP System
entry.content:
Hi All!

The content of this blog was initially written in Chinese by myself (Not AI), but later it was translated into English with the help of AI, and the AI also enhanced and structured the content. And the content reflects my own experiences and thoughts, and includes both text and images.


I'm a bit of a newcomer when it comes to posting here — but definitely not a stranger to here. I've been lurking and learning from your blogs for over a decade, and countless threads here have saved me in real-world projects. So thank you all!

Recently, I built something that genuinely excited me, and I wanted to share it with this community. This post is about how I explored AI Agents, connected them with SAP, and ultimately built a fully custom MCP Server natively inside SAP ABAP — enabling AI Agents to interact directly with SAP business processes using natural language.

 

Background: The Mission

Back in early January this year, my team lead gave me an open-ended challenge: while continuing to support our current work, explore how AI can drive business innovation.

I dove in. Here's what I explored:

  • Set up a local AI model (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) on my Windows PC refering the ModelScope.
  • Built a simple Python-based Chat Agent with VS Code extensions (Lingma/Copilot)
  • Created a local MCP Server (Python-based using Lingma/Copilot) with tools for things like: checking GPU status, logging into SAP, downloading email attachments from Outlook, detecting invoice files and auto-sorting them

The result: I could have natural language conversations with the AI, and it would intelligently call these tools to get things done. Pretty cool!

 

The First Breakthrough — and the Problem It Revealed

After building the local MCP Server, I started thinking: what if I could make the AI Agent interact directly with SAP?

I added a tool to my local MCP Server that called an SAP API. It worked! The AI Agent could now talk to SAP. I was thrilled.

But then reality hit.

Every single SAP API endpoint required:

  1. Creating a new API endpoint on the SAP side
  2. Adding a new tool in the Python MCP Server to call that endpoint
  3. Manually handling and exposing all the input parameters

Scale that to hundreds or thousands of business scenarios, and the maintenance burden would be enormous. Worse, the tight coupling between the Python MCP Server and the SAP system felt architecturally wrong. I wanted something more elegant — a decoupled, generalized framework.

So the question became: What if I built the MCP Server directly inside SAP?

 

The Solution: A Native SAP ABAP MCP Server

Learning MCP First

Before building anything, I went back to basics. I studied the Model Context Protocol (MCP) from the official documentation. And combined with video tutorials, I got a clear picture of how MCP works: it's a standard protocol that defines how AI Agents discover and call tools, enabling LLMs to interact with external systems in a structured way.

System Architecture

Here's the high-level architecture I designed:

All MCP interactions — initialization, tool listing, and tool invocation — go through a single SAP ICF endpoint. The SAP system itself acts as the MCP Server.

 

Key ABAP Components

Here is a breakdown of the core classes and their roles:

The ICF HTTP Handler Class

Registered as an ICF service in SICF, this class is the entry point. It receives all incoming HTTP requests from the AI Agent and delegates them to the MCP Server.

The Core MCP Server Class

This is the brain. It handles:

  • Tool registration (In CONSTRUCTOR via the Tool Registry Class)
  • Request parsing 
  • MCP Initialization handshake 
  • Tool listing 
  • Tool invocation
  • JSON response building for the AI Agent

The Tool Registry Class

A central registry where all MCP tools are registered. When the MCP Server initializes, it queries this registry to build the tool catalog that gets exposed to AI Agents.

The Tool Interface

A standard ABAP interface that every tool class must implement. It defines:

  • Tool metadata: name, description (sent to the LLM so it knows when to use this tool)
  • Input schema: parameter definitions (name, type, description, required flag)
  • Execution logic: how to process the AI Agent's request and return a result

A Demo Tool Class

A sample tool that queries customer data. It demonstrates the full pattern:

  1. Define tool name and description
  2. Define input schema 
  3. Receive input from the AI Agent, parse it into ABAP fields
  4. Execute ABAP business logic
  5. Serialize result as JSON and return it up the chain: MCP Server → HTTP Handler → Agent → LLM → User

Key Design Principle: One Endpoint to Rule Them All

The most important design decision here is that the entire MCP protocol is served through a single ICF endpoint. Whether the AI Agent is initializing, requesting the tool list, or calling a specific tool — it all goes to the same URL. The MCP Server internally routes based on the method name in the request payload.

This dramatically simplifies the SAP-side setup. Adding a new business tool means:

  1. Create a new ABAP class implementing the tool interface.
  2. Register it in configuration table.
  3. Done — no new ICF services, no new HTTP configurations.

💡The LLM automatically discovers all registered tools through the tool listing handshake. You define the tool's description carefully, and the model figures out when to call it.

Funny work: You can try to use AI coding tools (Lingma/Copilot) to help write ABAP classes based on the Key ABAP Components part.

 

Results & Demo

By late January / early February, I had a fully functional, self-built SAP MCP Server running in our SAP system. I demoed it to the team, and in the following two months I refined it further, while also exploring broader Agent application topics like RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), Skills, and enterprise-grade Agent frameworks.

For demo screenshots showing real AI Agent ↔ SAP interactions, please refer the belows:

 

AI Agent Applications Used for Testing

I used existing AI Agent products as the client-side interface — no custom frontend needed:

  • GitHub Copilot CLI
  • Dify Platform
  • Codebuddy CLI
  • AnythingLLM

All of these support the MCP protocol, so they connect to the SAP MCP Server out of the box. Download and install any of them to try this yourself.

 

 

What's Next?

This is just the beginning. Some areas I'm actively exploring:

  • Expanding the tool library with more business-relevant SAP tools
  • Connecting multiple SAP systems through a federated MCP Server pattern
  • Integrating RAG to give the AI Agent access to SAP documentation and business knowledge

 

Closing Thoughts

I built this because I wanted to prove that SAP and modern AI Agents can work together natively — without heavy middleware or expensive third-party platforms. The MCP standard makes this surprisingly achievable even from a pure ABAP background.

If you're an ABAPer curious about AI, or an AI enthusiast wondering how to connect LLMs to SAP — I hope this inspires you to experiment. The barrier is lower than you think.

Would love to hear your thoughts, questions, or ideas in the comments. Let's explore this together! 🚀

entry.title: Start Small. Think at Scale. Business Professionals Can Use SAP & Claude to Build a Business Case.
entry.content:
Note: This blog provides an actionable step-by-step proof of concept (POC) for the SAP and Anthropic partnership that a business professional can build today using SAP's free Business Accelerator Hub sandbox data and Claude. The Master Prompt is included below. A sample output is provided in the Addendum. Already familiar with this context? Skip directly to The Proof of Concept section.

Build the POC. Create the Business Case. Start the Conversation.

The recent SAP and Anthropic partnership announcement ignited a new wave of opportunities. It got me thinking about how the combined capabilities of Claude and SAP will strengthen enterprises around the globe.


At the same time, I found myself wondering. How can Business Professionals add real value to what needs to happen to put these capabilities in place for maximum success?

It reminded me of a simple metaphor. Imagine digging a new swimming pool with a shovel. You are making progress. Slowly. Painfully. Then someone pulls up with a bulldozer. The hole that would have taken weeks gets done in a day. That is the uplift the SAP and Anthropic partnership represents for organizations who are ready to act.

So how can Business Professionals help jumpstart the process?

Consider the impact of creating a simple proof of concept using SAP's own sandbox data and Claude, grounded in business case best practices, to help prioritize initiatives that can create compelling value.

The Market Opportunity

The scale of this partnership is significant. SAP and Anthropic are not simply connecting two products. They are building an Autonomous Enterprise vision where Claude powered agents operate through SAP Business AI Platform, coordinate across SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, and other SAP products via Model Context Protocol (MCP), and are accessible to users through Joule.

IDC projects AI spending driven by agentic AI will reach $1.3 trillion by 2029, growing at 31.9% year over year. In a best case scenario Gartner projects a $450 billion enterprise AI opportunity by 2035. Yet according to the DSAG Investment Report published in February 2026, among SAP customers already using AI, only 3% are running SAP Business AI in production today. The opportunity is enormous. But before Business Professionals can seize it they must consider creating POCs and business cases that will move their organization from insight to action.

The New API Policy. A Quality Framework.

It is worth highlighting a recent SAP announcement that is directly relevant to this partnership. SAP's API policy governs how autonomous external agents make sequential API calls into SAP systems.

Consider this approach as a quality framework.

Think Lean Six Sigma, ITIL, or ISO 9001 for mission critical Cloud ERP processes. Controlled variation. Trusted inputs. Governed outputs.

Claude operating inside SAP's approved architecture brings these same quality principles to agentic AI. As a Six Sigma certified practitioner I can say with confidence that controlled variation and governed outputs are not optional in mission critical processes. They are the standard.

Errors in enterprise data cannot be fully eliminated but they must be managed, minimized, and controlled continuously. They stem from uncontrolled inputs, ungoverned processes, and systems operating outside defined boundaries.

SAP's framework controls that variation at the source, reducing risk to the lowest achievable level and keeping mission critical processes operating with the precision enterprises depend on.

From Insight to Action. Building Your Case

Consider this scenario. You are a Business Professional who has just learned about the SAP and Anthropic partnership. You see the opportunity clearly. You know your organization has processes that AI can benefit, especially with Claude and SAP working together.

But where do you start?

You are not a developer. You do not have a project budget approved. You cannot stand up a Joule environment this afternoon.

What you do have is business knowledge, SAP familiarity, and a growing confidence in what Claude can do. Most importantly you have a clear sense of where the pain and inefficiencies are in your organization today.

That is enough to get started.

The path forward has two parallel tracks that reinforce each other:

  1. Delivering a proof of concept (POC) that shows what is possible
  2. Creating a business case that justifies the investment to make it real at scale.

The good news is that SAP has already given you the tools to do both.

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub was built explicitly to accelerate customer time to value. Free to access. No SAP license required. No IT ticket needed. Just a free SAP ID and a clear business question you want Claude to help answer.

The proof of concept you build today becomes the evidence that funds the enterprise deployment tomorrow. The business case you build around it gives your organization's executive sponsors the confidence to act.

This approach is how Business Professionals provide meaningful leadership, collaborating with IT counterparts and key stakeholders to move from insight to action.

The Proof of Concept  

This POC is intentionally simple and uses SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox. It is a minimum viable product (MVP) approach designed to show what is possible without requiring engineering support, IT involvement, or a live SAP system. Every step is something a Business Professional can execute independently.

What you are about to build is a working example of agentic AI. Claude is not simply answering a question. It is reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition data, identifying risk, prioritizing action, and recommending next steps. That is agentic AI in action. And it is a preview of what runs inside Joule at enterprise scale.

The following steps walk you through the POC from start to finish.

What You Do

  1. Register for a free SAP ID at api.sap.com. Takes a few minutes.
  2. Go to the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This is the current OData V4 Purchase Order API for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and one example of the many sandbox APIs available across SAP's core product portfolio.
  3. Click “Try Out” at the top. Select “Sandbox” from the dropdown on the right side. Navigate to the Purchase Order section in the left hand navigation. Click the /PurchaseOrder “GET” option. Click “Run.”
  4. Copy the JSON response that appears under “Response Body.”
  5. Open Claude at claude.ai. Paste the data and enter the Master Prompt below. Note: A free Claude account is sufficient for this POC. Claude Pro provides higher usage limits if needed.

What Claude Does

  1. Reads and interprets the SAP S/4HANA Cloud purchase order data.
  2. Identifies which exceptions represent the highest business risk.
  3. Assigns a risk score to each record with a clear rationale.
  4. Recommends specific actions for each prioritized item.
  5. Produces a scored table ready for your business case conversation.
  6. Offers to draft a supplier communication for the highest risk item.

Master Prompt

Copy and paste the following prompt into Claude along with your JSON data (as noted above). You are reviewing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition purchase order data retrieved directly from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox via the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001).

[START PROMPT]

Review the records provided and analyze the following fields: PurchaseOrder number, Supplier, CreationDate, PurchaseOrderDate, PurchasingProcessingStatus, PurchaseOrderDeletionCode, ReleaseIsNotCompleted, and PurchasingReleaseStrategy.

Based on your analysis:

  1. Identify purchase orders that require attention. Focus on deletion flagged records, incomplete releases, unusual processing statuses, and supplier concentration patterns.
  2. Assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low to each flagged record with a brief rationale.
  3. Recommend a specific action for each flagged item.
  4. Produce a prioritized table with the following columns: PO Number, Supplier, Creation Date, Processing Status, Deletion Flag, Release Complete, Priority Level, Recommended Action.
  5. Provide a two to three sentence plain English summary of your overall findings before the table.
  6. After the table offer to draft a supplier communication for the highest priority item.
  7. Keep your total response concise. Summary, table, and offer only. No additional commentary unless asked.

[END PROMPT]

What You Just Built

A working example of agentic AI reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data. Built by a Business Professional. Using SAP's own tools. View the sample output in the Addendum below

This is your building block. The cornerstone of your business case. And the start of a meaningful conversation with your IT counterparts, key stakeholders, and executive sponsors about what is possible and the value this use case can deliver.

Start small. Think at scale.

From POC to Production

This POC uses the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox as a starting point outside of SAP's production architecture. That is intentional. The goal is to demonstrate the concept quickly and accessibly. Free sandbox data. No infrastructure required.

Joule Studio is where developers and business teams can go next. A governed, enterprise grade environment where Business Professionals and developers collaborate to build both pre-built and custom agents tailored to their specific processes, data, and workflows. Joule Studio requires SAP BTP, a Joule subscription, SAP Build licensing, and IT provisioning to activate. It is the right next step. See Activate Joule Studio in 5 Simple Steps for full prerequisites.  

Complementary approaches. Each with its own role.

In production this same capability runs natively inside SAP Business AI Platform through the MCP Gateway. It is accessible to users through Joule. Everything you just demonstrated is the reasoning capability that sits behind the potential production implementation. The logic transfers directly.

In this POC the Hub provides everything you need to show what is possible.

Building the Business Case

A strong business case requires the right elements addressed with clarity and evidence. The POC you just built directly addresses two of the most critical. It defines the problem in concrete terms and demonstrates measurable value, ROI potential, and a clear view of what is possible.

The implementation path and risk and governance elements are addressed by SAP's approved architecture. The path runs through SAP Business AI Platform, Joule, and the production framework SAP and Anthropic have built together.

That represents a credible and governed foundation for any business case conversation.


Security and Data You Can Depend On

The same security and governance framework that protects your SAP environment today protects every action Claude takes inside it. The controls do not change because AI is involved. They extend to cover it.

Every action is governed by the same role-based access controls your organization operates under today. Every decision Claude makes is traceable through a full audit trail.

Connecting external data sources to the SAP environment requires proper integration through the SAP Integration Suite, ensuring data flows through governed, secure pathways before Claude ever touches it.

The Path Forward

The SAP and Anthropic partnership is designed for enterprise scale, grounded in trust and quality outcomes. Today, Business Professionals can create a tangible starting point and play a leadership role in the conversation with IT and business stakeholders about initiatives that can create lasting value.

SAP and Anthropic have announced plans to bring Claude to SAP Business AI Platform. Joule Studio, the development environment where agents are built and deployed, has general availability planned for later this year.

Now is the time to build your POC and business case so your organization is ready.

Take it one step at a time.

Start small. Think at scale.

Join the Conversation

Your likes and comments are welcome and appreciated.

  • Are you excited about the SAP and Anthropic partnership?
  • Were you aware of the SAP Business Accelerator Hub?
  • What are you working on with Claude or your favorite Assistant AI that can be applied to the Autonomous Enterprise vision?

About the Author

Rich Blumberg is an award-winning writer and author of two Amazon published books, Novi’s Journey to the Magic Garden and Job Seeking Warriors – A Mentors Guide to Winning. He's the President of World Sales Solutions, LLC, (WSS) a management consulting, low-code, no-code, and AI services firm. For more than 20 years he has worked as an SAP consultant partnering with SAP and their ecosystem organizations around the globe. Outside of work Rich serves as a Drexel University Alumni Board of Governors Emeritus, an ENGin English tutor supporting Ukrainian citizens, and an aspiring guitar player.

Related Blogs

  • How the AI Content Lifecycle Turns Complexity into Clarity at Scale(Part 2)
  • AI-Powered Knowledge Gap Analysis and Content Lifecycle Governance(Part 1)
  • You Can Build an Agentic AI Workflow. No Coding, No Engineering, No Problem.
  • AI at Warp Speed: Lessons from the Horse and Buggy Collapse

Addendum SAP S/4HANA Cloud POC Output

Purchase Order Analysis using the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox and Claude

Summary

Of the 50 purchase orders reviewed, 6 require immediate attention. Two records carry both a deletion flag and an incomplete release status, representing the highest risk. Four additional records show a deletion code with a completed processing status, suggesting they were cancelled but may warrant confirmation. Supplier 17300001 dominates the dataset with over 60% concentration, which itself warrants a supplier risk review.

Note: In a production environment this same analysis can run across thousands of purchase orders in seconds.

Prioritized Purchase Order Exception Table

Draft Supplier Communication

Highest Priority Item: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 | Supplier USSU-VSF01

Subject: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 – Urgent Status Clarification Required

Dear USSU-VSF01 Team,

I am writing regarding two purchase orders currently showing an unresolved status in our SAP S/4HANA system that require your immediate attention.

Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022, both dated August 18, 2016, are currently flagged for deletion while the approval release process remains incomplete. This combination of statuses prevents us from properly closing or reactivating these orders and creates a compliance risk in our procurement records.

We kindly request that you confirm the following by end of business this week:

  1. Whether any goods or services were delivered against either purchase order.
  2. Whether any outstanding invoices or payment obligations remain.
  3. Your agreement to formally close both orders if no outstanding obligations exist.

Please contact our procurement team at your earliest convenience so we can resolve this matter and ensure our records accurately reflect the current state of our relationship.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request.

Best regards,
Procurement Team
Company Code 1710

Data source: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) via SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox. Analysis performed by
